count_or_scope: 7 artifact families in refined vendor inventory summary.
evidence: Seven artifact families: source/IP, simulation/verification, constraint/configuration, transformation state, report/signoff, bitstream/programming, CI/CD release provenance.
how_it_selects_records: Only candidates tied to one of these artifact families can be retained.

count_or_scope: 1114 refined artifact rows across two controlled designs.
evidence: Vivado, Quartus Prime Pro, and Libero SoC generated artifacts plus vendor-documented flow vocabulary cited in the manuscript.
how_it_selects_records: Retained records must map to native tool evidence or explicit project-level provenance evidence.

evidence: excluded_generic_IT=8; excluded_no_evidence_requirement=2; excluded_physical_runtime=7; merged_duplicate=11; retained_distinct=18; rewritten_to_retained=16
how_it_selects_records: A candidate is retained only if it satisfies artifact, boundary, attacker capability, evidence, and mitigation criteria.

count_or_scope: Generic STRIDE, FPGA literature themes, FPGA flow prompts, observed artifact families, supply-chain prompts, and speculative prompts.
evidence: Artifact-provenance derivation=18; Generic STRIDE prompt=14; FPGA flow prompt=11; FPGA security literature theme=10; Supply-chain prompt=4; Observed artifact family=3; Speculative prompt=2
how_it_selects_records: Generic items are rewritten, duplicate low-level scenarios are merged, and physical/runtime or no-evidence candidates are excluded.

count_or_scope: Six controlled-revision runs expanded into twelve perturbation trace rows.
evidence: hdl_revision:AMD Vivado=traceable; hdl_revision:Intel Quartus Prime Pro=traceable; hdl_revision:Microchip Libero SoC=traceable; constraint_revision:AMD Vivado=traceable; constraint_revision:Intel Quartus Prime Pro=traceable; constraint_revision:Microchip Libero SoC=traceable
how_it_selects_records: Controlled revisions check whether source and constraint changes produce traceable downstream evidence.

lesson_for_this_paper: Make the validation object explicit; do not claim operational risk reduction without field data.
selection_or_evaluation_basis: Enterprise threat-modeling deployment and operational logs.
what_it_validates: Adoption, perceived usefulness, mitigations, and operational outcomes.

lesson_for_this_paper: A method paper needs an explicit protocol and outcome unit even when it does not demonstrate attacks.
selection_or_evaluation_basis: Industrial case-study comparison of threat-analysis procedures.
what_it_validates: Comparative productivity, prioritization, and scenario quality.

lesson_for_this_paper: Do not claim analyst usefulness or completeness without participant data.
selection_or_evaluation_basis: Multiple empirical studies over analysis tasks.
what_it_validates: Correctness, completeness, productivity, ease of use, and reliability.

lesson_for_this_paper: If using literature as a basis, state the coding scope and do not overclaim PRISMA-grade systematic review.
what_it_validates: Threat-modeling method categories and validation practices.

lesson_for_this_paper: A worked use case is valid when the claim is domain reinterpretation rather than effectiveness.
selection_or_evaluation_basis: Domain asset decomposition and worked AI/ML use case.
what_it_validates: Domain-specific STRIDE reinterpretation and mitigation mapping.

lesson_for_this_paper: STRIDE adaptation alone is not novel; the FPGA artifact/provenance schema must carry novelty.
selection_or_evaluation_basis: Review and classification of STRIDE-based domain methodologies.
what_it_validates: How STRIDE is adapted across domains.

lesson_for_this_paper: This paper cannot claim taxonomy effectiveness without human evaluation; use artifact-case validation instead.
what_it_validates: Taxonomy effect on relevant threats found by analysts.

lesson_for_this_paper: Use an operational schema, explicit baseline, and case evidence; here the baseline is generic STRIDE and the cases are tool/release artifacts.
selection_or_evaluation_basis: Tool specialization, incident/schema mapping, and baseline comparison.
what_it_validates: Domain schema coverage against real incidents and a baseline tool.
